---
title: "Update of the Indian Ocean albacore Operating Model"
subtitle: "IOTC Working Party on Methods MSE Taskforce"
date: "01-05 March 2021"
author: Iago MOSQUEIRA
institute: Wageningen Marine Research (WMR), IJmuiden. The Netherlands.
output:
  wmrkdown::wur
fontsize: 11pt
tags: [ALB albacore Indian Ocean IOTC OM operating model SS3]
---

```{r,setup, include=FALSE}
library(knitr)
opts_chunk$set(cache=TRUE, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", out.width="70%")
```

# Outline

- 3rd iteration of albacore OM.
- Based on WPTmT 2019 SS3 model.
- Existing FLR platform (`ss3om` + `ioalbmse` + `mse`) for SS 3.30.16.
- Contract WMR - IOTC/FAO August 2020 - December 2021.
- `https://github.com/iotcwpm/alb` (to be updated)


# WPTmT 2016 ALB OM grid

| Factor              | N | Prod | Values                   |
|---------------------|---|------|--------------------------|
| Natural mortality   | 5 | 5    | 0202, 0303, 0404, 0403, 0402 |
| Steepness SRR       | 3 | 15   | 0.7, 0.8, 0.9            |
| sigma recruitment   | 2 | 30   | 0.4, 0.6                 |
| ESS length comps.   | 3 | 90   | 20, 50, 100              |
| CV CPUE             | 4 | 360  | 0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.5       |
| LL q increase       | 2 | 720  | 0%, 0.25% per quarter    |
| Selectivity form    | 2 | 1440 | logistic, double normal  |

- 1,440 SS3 model runs (1.5 h each)


# WPTmT 2016 ALB OM

```{r}
include_graphics("../figures/om2016.png")
```

# Analysis of 2016 grid

- What factors determine production, variability and status
- Adult M and ESS + CPUECV most important, then LLq

```{r, out.width="45%", fig.show="hold", fig.align="default"}
include_graphics(c("../figures/rec.png", "../figures/prodfun_OM.png"))
```

# Regression tree SB0

```{r, out.width="65%"}
include_graphics("../data/regtree_ssb0.png")
```

# The WPTmT 2019 SS3 albacore stock assessment

- 2 sex, one area
- 4 LL fleets, DN (1982-92), PS (NW), Other.
- 3 runs: NW CPUE + LF, SW CPUE + LF, NW CPUE down LF

```{r, out.height="60%"}
include_graphics("../data/runs_wp.png")
```

# Fleet spatial structure

![](../figures/areas_albacore.png){ width=45% }
![](../data/index_selex_2017.png){ width=45% }

# The WPTmT 2019 SS3 albacore stock assessment

```{r}
include_graphics("../data/runs_wp_2014.png")
```

# Model diagnostics: Retrospective analysis

```{r}
include_graphics("../base/retro_SSB.png")
```

# Model diagnostics: CPUE runs tests

```{r}
include_graphics("../base/runs_CPUE.png")
```

# Model diagnostics: LF runs tests

```{r}
include_graphics("../base/runs_LF.png")
```

# Model diagnostics: Hindcasting cross validation (MASE)

![](../base/mase1.png){ width=45% }
![](../base/mase3.png){ width=45% }

- MASE < 1: LLCPUE3 S1, S4, LLCPUE1 S1, S4

# Parameter uncertainty: MVLN

```{r}
include_graphics("../base/kobe.png")
```

# Operating Model grid

- Natural mortality (M):
  - 0.20, 0.25, 0.30 or 0.35, for all ages.
- SD recruitment deviates (sigmaR):
  - 0.4, 0.6, or 0.8.
- SRR steepness (h):
  - 0.7, 0.8 or 0.9.
- LL CPUE series (cpues):
  - Northwest (LLCPUE1, 12) or Southwest (LLCPUE3, 14).
- LF data lkhd weighting (lfreq):
  - 0.01, 0.1 or 1.
- Catchability increase LL CPUE (llq):
  - 0% or 1% per year.

- 4 x 3 x 3 x 2 x 3 x 2 = 432 runs

# Main effects: Change in SB0, SBMSY by factor

![](../maineffects/ssb0.png){ width=45% } ![](../maineffects/ssbmsy.png){ width=45% } 

# Grid corners

- 64 (2^6) model runs:
  - 3 with $B_0 > 1e7$.
  - 1 with final gradient > 1e-4.
  - 8 with MASE(LLCPUE3S01) > 1
- 53 model runs accepted

# Regression tree

```{r}
include_graphics("../corners/regtrees.png")
```

# MASE by LLCPUE and season

\centering![](../corners/mases.png){ width=65% }

# Weighting of OM runs

- Diebold-Mariano test (Harvey, Leybourne and Newbold, 1997).
- Compares naive and model 1-step-ahead predictions of CPUE.
  - prediction skill model > naive.
- DM p-value used as weight.

\centering![](../output/om_weights.png){ width=45% }

# OM 500 resamples

\centering![](../output/om_worm.png){ width=55% }

# Corners OM vs. base case SA

```{r}
include_graphics("../corners/om_base.png")
```

# OM factors variability vs. SA

```{r, out.width="99%"}
include_graphics("../output/factors_base_sbmsy.png")
include_graphics("../output/factors_base_status.png")
```

# Projection F=FMSY

```{r}
include_graphics("../output/fut_fmsy.png")
```

# OM difference by CPUE

```{r}
include_graphics("../output/om_cpues.png")
```

# Hindcast MPs on 2016 grid (2000-2014)

- Schaefer, Fox & Pella ($B_{MSY}/K = 0.20$)
- Ftarget = $F_{MSY}$ multiplier

```{r, out.height="65%"}
include_graphics("../figures/MPs.png")
```

# Production function (OM & MPs) by M, ESS and h

```{r, out.width="65%"}
include_graphics("../figures/prodfun_OM-MPs.png")
```

# Open questions on OM

- Maintain the two CPUES given only one will run MP?
  - Run MPs on LLCPU3 (SW) start of year (S04-S01).
  - Separate as 2 OMs?
- Likelihood lambda enough contrast CPUE ~ LF?
- Do corners suffice to cover uncertainty space?
- Weighting based on prediction skill.
- Recruitment deviates to follow recent trends?
- Base case on extreme of OM: status.

# Next steps

- Biomass dynamics MP implemented.
  1. TMB Jabba-like (observation error, fixed process error).
  2. Jabba slim down, 30s running time.
- Capture the spatial component in CPUE in one area OM?


# Simulations workplan

- Tune MPs for
  1. P(Kobe == green 2030-2034) = 50%
  2. P(Kobe == green 2030-2034) = 60%
  3. P(Kobe == green 2030-2034) = 70%
- Robustness tests
  - Continuing recruitment decline
  - LLCPUE overcompensation bias 
  - 10% overcatch, reported
  - 10% overcatch, not reported

# Full grid vs. corners in 2016 OM
\centering![](../data/corners_2016-full.png){ width=85% }

# Full grid vs. corners in 2016 OM

\centering![](../figures/mses_2016.png){ width=45% } ![](../figures/mses_2016-corners.png){ width=45% }

# Central Composite Design
\centering![](../figures/ccd.png){ width=85% }

- 2 ^ 6 + 1 + 2 * 6 = 77
- Compares well to 3 ^ 6 ([Sanchez, 2005]( https://doi.org/10.1145/1113316.1113320))

# Corners + main effects

\centering![](../om/fuller-om_base.png){ width=85% }

# Corners by CPUE vs. bases

\centering![](../om/oms_bases.png){ width=85% }

# Recruitment residuals in projections

\centering![](../om/fut_recs.png){ width=55% } 

# Recruitment residuals in projections

\centering![](../om/pred-recs.png){ width=55% }
